content:
On Sunday morning, several low-flying, slow-moving objects were detected in Finnish airspace over sea areas and Southeast Finland. The Finnish Air Force deployed an F/A-18 Hornet fighter for identification. According to current information, two drones have crashed: one north of Kouvola and another east of Kouvola. Police have secured the crash sites for further investigation.

Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen confirmed that the drones strayed into Finnish territory, stating that the incident is being taken very seriously and that security authorities responded immediately. The Defence Forces are currently investigating the incident together with other authorities.
